---
title: "Section 2 Analysis"
author: "Suryadyuti Baral"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
source("Rfunctions.R")
result_tbl2 <- readRDS("result_tbl2.rds")
result_tbl_var <- readRDS("result_tbl2_var.rds")
```


```{r}
# Step 1: Remove seed column and reshape to long format
result_long <- result_tbl2 %>%
  dplyr::select(-1) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "condition", values_to = "content")

# Step 2: Identify completely empty lists
result_long_processed <- result_long %>%
  mutate(
    is_empty_list = map_lgl(content, ~ is.null(.x) || !is.list(.x) || length(.x) == 0),
    Metrics = map(content, ~ {
      if (is.list(.x) && "Metrics" %in% names(.x)) .x$Metrics else NULL
    })
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(condition, Metrics, is_empty_list)

# Step 3: Count how many empty lists per condition and list row numbers
empty_counts <- result_long_processed %>%
  mutate(seed_group = (row_number() - 1) %/% 18 + 1) %>%
  filter(is_empty_list) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    empty_count = n(),
    seeds = paste(seed_group, collapse = ","),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Step 4: Unnest non-empty Metrics
metrics_df <- result_long_processed %>%
  filter(!is_empty_list) %>%
  dplyr::select(-is_empty_list) %>%
  unnest(Metrics)

# Step 5: Summarize metrics by condition with values > 10^5 replaced by NA
summary_stats <- metrics_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(condition, subject), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    value = ifelse(abs(value) > 1e5, NA, value),  # Replace large values with NA
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c(
      "MSE_FDA", "Bias_FDA", "ISE_FDA", "IE_FDA",
      "MSE_LCS", "Bias_LCS", "ISE_LCS", "IE_LCS"
    ))
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition, metric) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = signif(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = signif(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition, metric)


empty_counts
summary_stats
```
```{r}
# remove seed column from both
res2 <- result_tbl2 %>% dplyr::select(-1)
res_var <- result_tbl_var %>% dplyr::select(-1)

# conditions assumed to match
conditions <- intersect(names(res2), names(res_var))

mse_var_df <- map_dfr(conditions, function(cond) {
  
  n_rep <- min(nrow(res2), nrow(res_var))
  
  map_dfr(seq_len(n_rep), function(i) {
    
    mse_obj <- res2[[cond]][[i]]
    var_obj <- res_var[[cond]][[i]]
    
    # skip failed / malformed reps
    if (is.null(mse_obj) || 
        !is.list(mse_obj) ||
        !"Metrics" %in% names(mse_obj) ||
        is.null(var_obj) ||
        !"variance" %in% names(var_obj)) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    variance <- var_obj$variance
    
    # protect against bad variance
    if (!is.finite(variance) || variance <= 0) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    metrics <- mse_obj$Metrics
    
    tibble(
      condition = cond,
      replication = i,
      subject = metrics$subject,
      MSE_FDA_scaled = metrics$MSE_FDA / variance,
      MSE_LCS_scaled = metrics$MSE_LCS / variance,
      variance = variance
    )
  })
})

# summarize
summary_scaled_mse <- mse_var_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(MSE_FDA_scaled, MSE_LCS_scaled),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    value = ifelse(abs(value) > 1e5, NA, value)
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition, metric) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = signif(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = signif(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition, metric)

summary_scaled_mse
```


```{r}
summary_stats_latex <- summary_stats %>%
  mutate(
    stat_latex = paste0(mean, " (", sd, ")")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(condition, metric, stat_latex) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = metric,
    values_from = stat_latex
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition)

write.csv(summary_stats_latex, "summary_stats_latex.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
n_time <- seq(0, 19, length.out = 240)
plot <- list()  

for (j in 2:ncol(result_tbl2)) {
  
  d_bound_fda_list <- list()
  d_bound_lcs_list <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(result_tbl2)) {
    cell <- result_tbl2[[i, j]][[1]]
    
    if (!(is.null(cell) || !is.list(cell) || length(cell) == 0)) {
      if (!is.null(cell$DB_FDA) && !is.null(cell$DB_LCS)) {
        d_bound_fda_list[[length(d_bound_fda_list) + 1]] <- cell$DB_FDA
        d_bound_lcs_list[[length(d_bound_lcs_list) + 1]] <- cell$DB_LCS
      }
    }
  }

  d_bound_fda <- do.call(cbind, d_bound_fda_list)
  d_bound_lcs <- do.call(cbind, d_bound_lcs_list)
  
  plot[[j - 1]] <- plot_d_bound_comparison(d_bound_fda, d_bound_lcs, n_time)
}

names(plot) <- names(result_tbl2)[2:ncol(result_tbl2)]


```

```{r}
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# Helper function to strip axis labels and add tiny subplot tag
strip_and_tag <- function(p, tag) {
  p + labs(title = NULL, x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    ggtitle(tag) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5, face = "plain"))
}

# Subplot names
top_names <- c("homogeneous_n100_tp2", "homogeneous_n250_tp2", "homogeneous_n500_tp2")
bottom_names <- c("homogeneous_n100_tp3", "homogeneous_n250_tp3", "homogeneous_n500_tp3")

# Apply stripping and tagging
top_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[1]]], top_names[1]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[2]]], top_names[2]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[3]]], top_names[3])

bottom_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[1]]], bottom_names[1]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[2]]], bottom_names[2]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[3]]], bottom_names[3])

# Combine all
combined_plot <- (top_row / bottom_row) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 500), breaks = c(0, 250, 500))

combined_plot <- combined_plot +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Width of the CI Comparison: Homogeneous Condition",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(r = 10))
    )
  ) 

combined_plot



```
```{r}
# Subplot names
top_names <- c("heterogeneous_n100_tp2", "heterogeneous_n250_tp2", "heterogeneous_n500_tp2")
bottom_names <- c("heterogeneous_n100_tp3", "heterogeneous_n250_tp3", "heterogeneous_n500_tp3")

# Apply stripping and tagging
top_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[1]]], top_names[1]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[2]]], top_names[2]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[3]]], top_names[3])

bottom_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[1]]], bottom_names[1]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[2]]], bottom_names[2]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[3]]], bottom_names[3])

# Combine all
combined_plot <- (top_row / bottom_row) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 500), breaks = c(0, 250, 500))

# Add global title and axis labels
combined_plot <- combined_plot +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Width of the CI Comparison: Heterogeneous Condition",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(r = 10))
    )
  ) 

combined_plot
```
```{r}
# Subplot names
top_names <- c("functional_heterogeneous_n100_tp2", "functional_heterogeneous_n250_tp2", "functional_heterogeneous_n500_tp2")
bottom_names <- c("functional_heterogeneous_n100_tp3", "functional_heterogeneous_n250_tp3", "functional_heterogeneous_n500_tp3")

# Apply stripping and tagging
top_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[1]]], top_names[1]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[2]]], top_names[2]) +
           strip_and_tag(plot[[top_names[3]]], top_names[3])

bottom_row <- strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[1]]], bottom_names[1]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[2]]], bottom_names[2]) +
              strip_and_tag(plot[[bottom_names[3]]], bottom_names[3])

# Combine all
combined_plot <- (top_row / bottom_row) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 500), breaks = c(0, 250, 500))

# Add global title and axis labels
combined_plot <- combined_plot +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Width of the CI Comparison: Functionally Heterogeneous Condition",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(r = 10))
    )
  ) 

combined_plot
```
